--- title: Mapping Celestia's Network execute: echo: false --- *"The Lord has been and is very kind to me in giving me so many friends may I ever be grateful for all his benefits."* **Celestia Webster's Diary - May 17, 1867** ## Celestia's Network in Livinston County ![1850 Map of Livingston County, New York](1850map.jpg){alt="1850 Map of Livingston County, New York" fig-alt="This is a 1850 map of Livingston County, New York." fig-align="center" width="502"} [![1858 Survey Map of Livingston County](gileslivingston.jpg){fig-alt="This is a survey map of Livingston County, New York with details on county boundaries, pop outs of important cities, and directory information" fig-align="center" width="678"}](https://www.loc.gov/resource/g3803l.la000515/?r=-0.21,-0.138,1.279,0.681,0) ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── ✔ dplyr 1.2.0 ✔ readr 2.2.0 ✔ forcats 1.0.1 ✔ stringr 1.6.0 ✔ ggplot2 4.0.2 ✔ tibble 3.3.1 ✔ lubridate 1.9.5 ✔ tidyr 1.3.2 ✔ purrr 1.2.1 ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() ✖ dplyr::lag() masks stats::lag() ℹ Use the conflicted package () to force all conflicts to become errors Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE Rows: 13 Columns: 6 ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr (3): address, city, diary location dbl (3): lat, lng, zip ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "library(sf)\nlibrary(tidygeocoder)\nlibrary(USAboundaries)\nlibrary(tidyverse)\n\ncnetwork <- read.csv(C_Network.csv)\n\nlocations <- geo(c(Conesus, Livonia, Springwater, Hemlock))\nlocations\n\nlocations_sf <- st_as_sf(cnetwork, coords = c(lng, lat), crs = 4326)\nlocations_sf\n\nnewyork <- us_counties(states = New York)\n\n# Find EPSG code for New York\nstate_plane(state = NY)\n\n# Project\nlocs_proj <- st_transform(locations_sf, EPSG:32146)\nnewyork_proj <- st_transform(newyork, EPSG:32116)\n\nggplot() + \n geom_sf(data = newyork_proj) + \n geom_sf(data = locs_proj) + \n labs(title = Celestia's Network in Livingston County)\n theme_minimal()\nlocation <- networklocations\n filter (column_ == diary location) - this is for point map\nggplot() + \n geom_sf(data = newyork_proj) + \n geom_sf(data = locs_proj) +\n coord_sf(xlim = c(-76.0,-78.0),ylim = c(43.0,42.0))" ``` ::: :::
